title: achillea alpina subsp. japonica
intro: Achillea alpina subsp. japonica, also known as Japanese alpine yarrow, is a subspecies of the yarrow plant that is native to Japan and the mountainous regions of East Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe digestive issues, as well as its potential to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Herbal preparations of Achillea alpina subsp. japonica can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
